184	1	name: Jira Ticket agent
185	2	
186	3	on:
187	4	  schedule:
188	5	    - cron: '17 7 * * *'

190	7	
191	8	jobs:
192	9	  groom:
193	10	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
194	11	    permissions:
195	12	      contents: write
196	13	      id-token: write
197	14	      actions: write
198	15	    steps:
199	16	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
200	17	        with:
201	18	          fetch-depth: 1
202	19	
203	20	      - name: Install jira CLI
204	21	        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh
205	22	
206	23	      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
207	24	        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
208	25	
209	26	      - name: Write jira config
210	27	        env:
211	28	          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
212	29	          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
213	30	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
214	31	          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
215	32	        run: |
216	33	          set -euo pipefail
217	34	          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
218	35	          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
219	36	          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
220	37	          mkdir -p .jira
221	38	          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
222	39	          installation: Cloud
223	40	          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
224	41	          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
225	42	          project:
226	43	              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
227	44	              type: classic
228	45	          board:
229	46	              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
230	47	          EOF
231	48	
232	49	      - id: prompt
233	50	        env:
234	51	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
235	52	        run: |
236	53	          {
237	54	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
238	55	            echo "JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}"
239	56	            echo
240	57	            cat agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
241	58	            echo PROMPT_EOF
242	59	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
243	60	
244	61	      - id: claude
245	62	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
246	63	        env:
247	64	          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
248	65	          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}

259	1	name: PR Review agent
260	2	
261	3	on:
262	4	  pull_request:
263	5	    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
264	6	    paths-ignore:
265	7	      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
266	8	      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
267	9	  workflow_dispatch:
268	10	
269	11	jobs:
270	12	  review:
271	13	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
272	14	    permissions:
273	15	      contents: write
274	16	      pull-requests: write
275	17	      id-token: write
276	18	      actions: write
277	19	    steps:
278	20	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
279	21	        with:
280	22	          fetch-depth: 1
281	23	
282	24	      - id: prompt
283	25	        run: |
284	26	          {
285	27	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
286	28	            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
287	29	            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
288	30	            echo
289	31	            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
290	32	            echo PROMPT_EOF
291	33	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
292	34	
293	35	      - id: claude
294	36	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
295	37	        with:
296	38	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
297	39	          track_progress: false
298	40	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
299	41	          claude_args: |
300	42	            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"
301	43	
302	44	      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
303	45	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
304	46	        env:
305	47	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
306	48	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
307	49	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
308	50	          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
309	51	        run: |
310	52	          set -e
311	53	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
312	54	          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
313	55	          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
314	56	          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
315	57	          DEST=".github/agen

324	```
325	1	# Agentic Development Workflow
326	2	
327	3	> **Status:** First-draft vision. Extracted from a hand-drawn diagram (2026-06-04).
328	4	> Not yet scoped, designed, or implemented.
329	5	
330	6	The flow describes an end-to-end pipeline where humans create tickets and merge
331	7	final PRs, and a chain of agents handles everything in between: ticket grooming,
332	8	complexity routing, implementation, testing, review, and refinement.
333	9	
334	10	## High-level flow
335	11	
336	12	```
337	13	Humans create tickets
338	14	        │
339	15	        ▼
340	16	   Ticket Agent
341	17	        │
342	18	        ▼
343	19	Development Orchestrator
344	20	        │
345	21	        ├──── complex ────► Senior developer agent  (/openspec:explore)
346	22	        │                          │
347	23	        │                          ▼
348	24	        │                    PR Review agent ◄────┐
349	25	        │                          ▲              │
350	26	        │                          ▼              │
351	27	        │                    PR Refinement agent  │
352	28	        │                          │              │
353	29	        │                          ▼              │
354	30	        │                Humans test & merge PR   │
355	31	        │                                         │
356	32	        └──── easy ──────► Junior developer agent │
357	33	                                  │   (/goal or /ralph-loop)
358	34	                                  ▼               │
359	35	                            Testing agent ────────┘
360	36	                          (loops back to dev
361	37	                           agent on failure)
362	38	```
363	39	
364	40	## Human touchpoints
365	41	
366	42	- **Start:** humans create tickets.
367	43	- **End:** humans test the final PR and merge it.
368	44	- **Mid-loop (complex path only):** the Senior developer agent pulls a human
369	45	  into the loop before implementing.
370	46	
371	47	## Agents
372	48	
373	49	### Ticket Agent
374	50	**Goal:** manage Jira tickets.
375	51	
376	52	- Does **not** invent new tickets.
377	53	- Works on a daily basis. 
378	54	- Decomposes tickets and improves descriptions based on the current code.
379	55	- Creates dependencies
380	```
381	
382	## Assistant
383	Now I have all the context I need. Let me assess each ticket:
384	
385	- **KAN-1**: In Progress, good description, correct line references (line 97-114 verified), two comments already resolved the only discrepancy. Leave alone.
386	- **KAN-2**: Has a broken log path in AC — `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev//.json` (double slash, missing `<DATE>/<RUN_ID>` segments). Fix needed.
387	- **KAN-3**: Well-formed, testable AC, correct path pattern. Leave alone.
388	- **KAN-4**: Well-formed, spec-driven flow clearly described with testable AC. Leave alone.
389	- **KAN-5**: Well-formed, references `pr-review.yml:57` (verified correct — that's the DEST line). Leave alone.
390	
391	One edit: fix KAN-2's broken AC path.
392	
393	## Bash
394	```
395	jira issue edit KAN-2 --no-input -b "Goal
396	Pick up a ticket flagged \"easy\" by the orchestrator, plan straight through to implementation in a headless Claude Code session, push a branch, open a PR.
397	
398	Decide in this ticket
399	Whether the prompt frames the work as /goal or /ralph-loop (both named in docs/agentic-workflow.md). Pick one and document why in the README.
400	
401	Deliverables
402	* agents/junior-dev/prompt.md — prompt structure matching agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md.
403	* agents/junior-dev/README.md.
404	* .github/workflows/junior-dev.yml — modeled on .github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml. Triggered via workflow_dispatch (with a ticket_key input) and/or repository_dispatch from the orchestrator.
405	
406	Open question to resolve
407	Stop condition so the loop cannot run forever on a hard ticket. Options:
408	- --max-turns N on the action.
409	- An attempt counter committed back to the ticket as a comment / label.
410	- Time-budget on the workflow.
411	Pick one and document the rationale.
412	
413	Acceptance criteria
414	* Dispatching the workflow with a real KAN ticket key produces a branch and a PR.
415	* Run log committed under .github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json (matching the jira-ticket convention at .github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml:97-114).
416	* The chosen stop condition is exercised in a deliberate failure scenario.
417	* The branch naming convention is defined in agents/junior-dev/README.md so .github/workflows/testing.yml can reference it as a pull_request filter."
418	```

Corrected to `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json`, consistent with the jira-ticket convention at `.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml:97-114`. A broken path in AC means two implementers would ship different log structures and the testing step would fail to find the log file.\n\n*What I skipped*\n• KAN-1: In Progress, description accurate (line refs 97-114 verified), two comments already resolved the only prior discrepancy — no action needed.\n• KAN-3: Well-formed; Goal, Deliverables, Loop-back mechanism, and AC all present and testable.\n• KAN-4: Well-formed; spec-driven flow and human-in-the-loop pause are clearly described with testable AC.\n• KAN-5: Well-formed; references `pr-review.yml:57` (verified — that's the `DEST` line with the correct log path pattern).\n"}

32	        run: |

34	          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
35	          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
36	          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
37	          mkdir -p .jira
38	          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
39	          installation: Cloud
40	          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
41	          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
42	          project:
43	              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
44	              type: classic
45	          board:
46	              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
47	          EOF
48	
49	      - id: prompt
50	        env:
51	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
52	        run: |
53	          {
54	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
55	            echo "JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}"
56	            echo
57	            cat agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
58	            echo PROMPT_EOF
59	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
60	
61	      - id: claude
62	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
63	        env:
64	          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
65	          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
66	     
```

## Tool: Read
```json
{"file_path":"/home/runner/work/my-harness/my-harness/.github/workflows/pr-review.yml"}
```

## Result
```
1	name: PR Review agent
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
6	    paths-ignore:
7	      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
8	      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
9	  workflow_dispatch:
10	
11	jobs:
12	  review:
13	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
14	    permissions:
15	      contents: write
16	      pull-requests: write
17	      id-token: write
18	      actions: write
19	    steps:
20	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
21	        with:
22	          fetch-depth: 1
23	
24	      - id: prompt
25	        run: |
26	          {
27	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
28	            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
29	            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
30	            echo
31	            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
32	            echo PROMPT_EOF
33	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
34	
35	      - id: claude
36	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
37	        with:
38	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
39	          track_progress: false
40	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
41	          claude_args: |
42	            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"
43	
44	      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
45	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
46	        env:
47	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
48	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
49	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
50	          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
51	        run: |
52	          set -e
53	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
54	          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
55	          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
56	          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
57	          DEST=".github/agen
```

## Result
```
1	# Agentic Development Workflow
2	
3	> **Status:** First-draft vision. Extracted from a hand-drawn diagram (2026-06-04).
4	> Not yet scoped, designed, or implemented.
5	
6	The flow describes an end-to-end pipeline where humans create tickets and merge
7	final PRs, and a chain of agents handles everything in between: ticket grooming,
8	complexity routing, implementation, testing, review, and refinement.
9	
10	## High-level flow
11	
12	```
13	Humans create tickets
14	        │
15	        ▼
16	   Ticket Agent
17	        │
18	        ▼
19	Development Orchestrator
20	        │
21	        ├──── complex ────► Senior developer agent  (/openspec:explore)
22	        │                          │
23	        │                          ▼
24	        │                    PR Review agent ◄────┐
25	        │                          ▲              │
26	        │                          ▼              │
27	        │                    PR Refinement agent  │
28	        │                          │              │
29	        │                          ▼              │
30	        │                Humans test & merge PR   │
31	        │                                         │
32	        └──── easy ──────► Junior developer agent │
33	                                  │   (/goal or /ralph-loop)
34	                                  ▼               │
35	                            Testing agent ────────┘
36	                          (loops back to dev
37	                           agent on failure)
38	```
39	
40	## Human touchpoints
41	
42	- **Start:** humans create tickets.
43	- **End:** humans test the final PR and merge it.
44	- **Mid-loop (complex path only):** the Senior developer agent pulls a human
45	  into the loop before implementing.
46	
47	## Agents
48	
49	### Ticket Agent
50	**Goal:** manage Jira tickets.
51	
52	- Does **not** invent new tickets.
53	- Works on a daily basis. 
54	- Decomposes tickets and improves descriptions based on the current code.
55	- Creates dependencies
```

## Assistant
Now I have all the context I need. Let me assess each ticket:

- **KAN-1**: In Progress, good description, correct line references (line 97-114 verified), two comments already resolved the only discrepancy. Leave alone.
- **KAN-2**: Has a broken log path in AC — `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev//.json` (double slash, missing `<DATE>/<RUN_ID>` segments). Fix needed.
- **KAN-3**: Well-formed, testable AC, correct path pattern. Leave alone.
- **KAN-4**: Well-formed, spec-driven flow clearly described with testable AC. Leave alone.
- **KAN-5**: Well-formed, references `pr-review.yml:57` (verified correct — that's the DEST line). Leave alone.

One edit: fix KAN-2's broken AC path.

## Bash
```
jira issue edit KAN-2 --no-input -b "Goal
Pick up a ticket flagged \"easy\" by the orchestrator, plan straight through to implementation in a headless Claude Code session, push a branch, open a PR.

Decide in this ticket
Whether the prompt frames the work as /goal or /ralph-loop (both named in docs/agentic-workflow.md). Pick one and document why in the README.

Deliverables
* agents/junior-dev/prompt.md — prompt structure matching agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md.
* agents/junior-dev/README.md.
* .github/workflows/junior-dev.yml — modeled on .github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml. Triggered via workflow_dispatch (with a ticket_key input) and/or repository_dispatch from the orchestrator.

Open question to resolve
Stop condition so the loop cannot run forever on a hard ticket. Options:
- --max-turns N on the action.
- An attempt counter committed back to the ticket as a comment / label.
- Time-budget on the workflow.
Pick one and document the rationale.

Acceptance criteria
* Dispatching the workflow with a real KAN ticket key produces a branch and a PR.
* Run log committed under .github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json (matching the jira-ticket convention at .github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml:97-114).
* The chosen stop condition is exercised in a deliberate failure scenario.
* The branch naming convention is defined in agents/junior-dev/README.md so .github/workflows/testing.yml can reference it as a pull_request filter."
```

## Result
```
[0;32m✓[0m Issue updated
https://jaeyoonsworkspace-23358853.atlassian.net/browse/KAN-2
```

## Tool: Write
```json
{"file_path":"/home/runner/work/my-harness/my-harness/slack-summary.md","content":"*What I did*\n• KAN-2: fixed broken log path in Acceptance Criteria\n\n*Why*\nThe AC contained `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev//.json` — a double-slash with missing `<DATE>/<RUN_ID>` segments, likely a templating artifact. Corrected to `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json`, consistent with the jira-ticket convention at `.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml:97-114`. A broken path in AC means two implementers would ship different log structures and the testing step would fail to find the log file.\n\n*What I skipped*\n• KAN-1: In Progress, description accurate (line refs 97-114 verified), two comments already resolved the only prior discrepancy — no action needed.\n• KAN-3: Well-formed; Goal, Deliverables, Loop-back mechanism, and AC all present and testable.\n• KAN-4: Well-formed; spec-driven flow and human-in-the-loop pause are clearly described with testable AC.\n• KAN-5: Well-formed; references `pr-review.yml:57` (verified — that's the `DEST` line with the correct log path pattern).\n"}
```

content.content:
1	You are the **Jira Ticket agent** for this repository. 
2	
3	## Your job
4	
5	Groom the configured Jira project. Edit existing tickets so the team starts
6	the day with a cleaner backlog. **Never create a top-level issue.**
7	
8	## Environment
9	
10	- `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and `JIRA_API_TOKEN` are already set.
11	- `jira` is on `PATH` (resolves to `bin/jira`, v1.7.0).
12	- Always pass `--no-input` on every mutating call — the CLI is interactive
13	  by default and will hang otherwise.
14	- Repo source is at CWD. Read it (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`) when you need code
15	  context to judge a description.
16	
17	## Scope (what to look at)
18	
19	Pull the open work for the configured project as JSON:
20	
21	```sh
22	jira issue list -q "project = $JIRA_PROJECT_KEY AND resolution = Unresolved" --raw
23	```
24	
25	That set is your full input for the run. Don't expand it.
26	
27	## Allowed mutations
28	
29	1. **Edit description** — when a description is vague, missing acceptance
30	   criteria, or contradicts what the code actually does:
31	
32	   ```sh
33	   jira issue edit <KEY> -b "<new description>" --no-input
34	   ```
35	
36	   Pass the description as a direct multi-line bash string — do not use
37	   `$()`, backtick substitution, or `printf` inside the `-b` value.
38	   Those patterns trigger shell-injection guards and will fail. A plain
39	   quoted string spread across lines is fine:
40	
41	   ```sh
42	   jira issue edit <KEY> --no-input -b "Line one
43	   Line two
44	   Line three"
45	   ```
46	
47	2. **Link related tickets** — when one clearly gates another:
48	
49	   ```sh
50	   jira issue link <BLOCKER> <BLOCKED> Blocks
51	   ```
52	
53	3. **Decompose an oversized parent into sub-tasks** — only as children of an
54	   existing ticket, never standalone:
55	
56	   ```sh
57	   jira issue create -tSub-task -p <PARENT_KEY> -s "<summary>" -b "<desc>" --no-input
58	   ```
59	
60	4. **Comment instead of mutating** — when you're not confident the change is
61	   an improvement:
62	
63	   ```sh
64	   jira issue comment add <KEY> "<note>" --no-input
65	   ```
66	
67	   Note: `comment add` takes the body as a positional argument, not `-b`.
68	   Using `-b` will fail with "unknown shorthand flag".
69	
70	## Ticket quality rubric
71	
72	Apply this when **rewriting a description** or **writing a new sub-task**.
73	A well-formed ticket has four parts:
74	
75	- **Title** *(sub-tasks only — never rename an existing summary)*: clear from
76	  a user/developer perspective. Concise. No type prefix like `Spike: …`,
77	  `Bug: …`, `Sub-task: …` — Jira already tracks the type field.
78	- **Description**: what exactly needs to be done, or what the problem is.
79	  Concrete, not aspirational. Reference real file paths or symbols from the
80	  working directory when they pin the work down.
81	- **Context**: why this matters — the technical reason or business value.
82	  One or two sentences. Skip if it's genuinely self-evident from the
83	  description; never pad.
84	- **Acceptance Criteria**: a short bullet list of testable conditions for
85	  "done." Each item should be something a reviewer can check, not a vague
86	  goal ("works correctly", "is clean").
87	
88	If you can't write a usable Context or AC for a sub-task you're about to
89	create, that's a signal the parent isn't decomposable yet — comment on the
90	parent instead of forcing a sub-task.
91	
92	## Forbidden
93	
94	- Creating a top-level issue. Every `jira issue create` call must be
95	  `-tSub-task` with `-p <PARENT>`.
96	- Transitioning, closing, or reassigning tickets.
97	- Touching anything not returned by the JQL above (no resolved/closed work).
98	- Bulk-editing every ticket. Only act when the change clearly helps.
99	
100	## When to edit vs leave alone
101	
102	Edit a description when measuring against the **Ticket quality rubric** above
103	shows at least one of:
104	- Missing AC on a task (one-liner, no testable conditions).
105	- References code that has since moved, been renamed, or no longer exists.
106	- Ambiguous enough that two engineers would build different things.
107	
108	## Stop criteria
109	
110	One pass over the JQL result set. No second pass, no retries on transient
111	errors beyond the obvious.
112	
113	## Final step: write a Slack summary file
114	
115	Before stopping, write your end-of-run summary to `slack-summary.md`
116	using the `Write` tool. A downstream workflow step posts it to Slack
117	verbatim via `chat.postMessage`, so write in **Slack mrkdwn**: `*bold*`
118	(single asterisks), `_italic_`, backticks for code. Do **not** use
119	`**double-asterisk**` markdown — Slack renders it as literal asterisks.
120	
121	Cover:
122	
123	- **What you did** — tickets touched, one bullet each as
124	  `KEY: <one-phrase action>` (e.g. `PROJ-123: rewrote description, added AC`).
125	- **Why** — 1–3 sentences naming the rubric gap you closed or the
126	  decomposition rationale. The reasoning, not a recap.
127	- **What you skipped** — tickets you deliberately left alone, with a
128	  one-line reason each. Skipping is information.
129	
130	Keep it under ~15 lines total. If you made no edits, write a single line
131	saying so plus why (e.g. "Backlog already meets the rubric — no action
132	this run"). Always write the file — even when there's nothing to report —
133	so the post step has something to send.
134	
135	Once the file is written, stop. Do not narrate or summarize further — the
136	Slack post is the run's output.
137

file.content:
You are the **Jira Ticket agent** for this repository. 

## Your job

Groom the configured Jira project. Edit existing tickets so the team starts
the day with a cleaner backlog. **Never create a top-level issue.**

## Environment

- `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and `JIRA_API_TOKEN` are already set.
- `jira` is on `PATH` (resolves to `bin/jira`, v1.7.0).
- Always pass `--no-input` on every mutating call — the CLI is interactive
  by default and will hang otherwise.
- Repo source is at CWD. Read it (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`) when you need code
  context to judge a description.

## Scope (what to look at)

Pull the open work for the configured project as JSON:

```sh
jira issue list -q "project = $JIRA_PROJECT_KEY AND resolution = Unresolved" --raw
```

That set is your full input for the run. Don't expand it.

## Allowed mutations

1. **Edit description** — when a description is vague, missing acceptance
   criteria, or contradicts what the code actually does:

   ```sh
   jira issue edit <KEY> -b "<new description>" --no-input
   ```

   Pass the description as a direct multi-line bash string — do not use
   `$()`, backtick substitution, or `printf` inside the `-b` value.
   Those patterns trigger shell-injection guards and will fail. A plain
   quoted string spread across lines is fine:

   ```sh
   jira issue edit <KEY> --no-input -b "Line one
   Line two
   Line three"
   ```

2. **Link related tickets** — when one clearly gates another:

   ```sh
   jira issue link <BLOCKER> <BLOCKED> Blocks
   ```

3. **Decompose an oversized parent into sub-tasks** — only as children of an
   existing ticket, never standalone:

   ```sh
   jira issue create -tSub-task -p <PARENT_KEY> -s "<summary>" -b "<desc>" --no-input
   ```

4. **Comment instead of mutating** — when you're not confident the change is
   an improvement:

   ```sh
   jira issue comment add <KEY> "<note>" --no-input
   ```

   Note: `comment add` takes the body as a positional argument, not `-b`.
   Using `-b` will fail with "unknown shorthand flag".

## Ticket quality rubric

Apply this when **rewriting a description** or **writing a new sub-task**.
A well-formed ticket has four parts:

- **Title** *(sub-tasks only — never rename an existing summary)*: clear from
  a user/developer perspective. Concise. No type prefix like `Spike: …`,
  `Bug: …`, `Sub-task: …` — Jira already tracks the type field.
- **Description**: what exactly needs to be done, or what the problem is.
  Concrete, not aspirational. Reference real file paths or symbols from the
  working directory when they pin the work down.
- **Context**: why this matters — the technical reason or business value.
  One or two sentences. Skip if it's genuinely self-evident from the
  description; never pad.
- **Acceptance Criteria**: a short bullet list of testable conditions for
  "done." Each item should be something a reviewer can check, not a vague
  goal ("works correctly", "is clean").

If you can't write a usable Context or AC for a sub-task you're about to
create, that's a signal the parent isn't decomposable yet — comment on the
parent instead of forcing a sub-task.

## Forbidden

- Creating a top-level issue. Every `jira issue create` call must be
  `-tSub-task` with `-p <PARENT>`.
- Transitioning, closing, or reassigning tickets.
- Touching anything not returned by the JQL above (no resolved/closed work).
- Bulk-editing every ticket. Only act when the change clearly helps.

## When to edit vs leave alone

Edit a description when measuring against the **Ticket quality rubric** above
shows at least one of:
- Missing AC on a task (one-liner, no testable conditions).
- References code that has since moved, been renamed, or no longer exists.
- Ambiguous enough that two engineers would build different things.

## Stop criteria

One pass over the JQL result set. No second pass, no retries on transient
errors beyond the obvious.

## Final step: write a Slack summary file

Before stopping, write your end-of-run summary to `slack-summary.md`
using the `Write` tool. A downstream workflow step posts it to Slack
verbatim via `chat.postMessage`, so write in **Slack mrkdwn**: `*bold*`
(single asterisks), `_italic_`, backticks for code. Do **not** use
`**double-asterisk**` markdown — Slack renders it as literal asterisks.

Cover:

- **What you did** — tickets touched, one bullet each as
  `KEY: <one-phrase action>` (e.g. `PROJ-123: rewrote description, added AC`).
- **Why** — 1–3 sentences naming the rubric gap you closed or the
  decomposition rationale. The reasoning, not a recap.
- **What you skipped** — tickets you deliberately left alone, with a
  one-line reason each. Skipping is information.

Keep it under ~15 lines total. If you made no edits, write a single line
saying so plus why (e.g. "Backlog already meets the rubric — no action
this run"). Always write the file — even when there's nothing to report —
so the post step has something to send.

Once the file is written, stop. Do not narrate or summarize further — the
Slack post is the run's output.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the run summary for concrete issues I can improve:

1. **The initial `jira issue list` returned 39.3KB**, which was too large to display in the context window. The agent had to do a follow-up `Read` on the persisted file. This is a failed-then-continued pattern. Could we improve this?
   - The prompt says to pull open work as JSON with `--raw`. The `--raw` flag returns the full JSON including all fields. If we could limit the fields, the output would be smaller.
   - But looking at the jira CLI, I'm not sure what flag would limit the output.
   - Actually, the agent didn't fail here—it just got a truncated view and then had to read the full file, which added an extra tool call.

2. **Redundant exploration** - After pulling the Jira issues, the agent ran 3 Glob commands and then read 3 files before even analyzing the tickets. This feels like unnecessary context gathering, but the prompt does say to read files when I need code context to judge a description, so the agent was following instructions.
   - The exploration wasn't entirely wasteful though—those files (`jira-ticket.yml`, `pr-review.yml`, and `docs/agentic-workflow.md`) were relevant to verifying the ticket descriptions, especially KAN-5 which referenced a specific line in `pr-review.yml`. The docs read was a bit more exploratory than necessary.

3. **The redundant file read** - The `jira issue list` command returned output that was too large, forcing the agent to read the persisted file as a second step. This is inefficient, but it's a CLI limitation rather than something I can fix at the prompt level.

4. **Agent wrote `slack-summary.md`** - this is the correct behavior per the prompt.

5. **The slack summary used `*bold*`** - which is correct per the prompt's instruction.

Looking at the overall flow, the key inefficiency was doing three glob and read operations upfront before analyzing any tickets. A better approach would be to read all tickets first, identify which files are referenced, then only read those specific files. But in this case, the exploration was fairly targeted since the agent read the most commonly referenced workflow and docs files, and the `pr-review.yml` read was justified by KAN-5's explicit reference to it.

The real issue worth addressing in the prompt is that the agent did broad upfront exploration rather than reading files on-demand for specific tickets. The prompt could be more explicit about deferring file reads until they're actually needed for analyzing a particular ticket. The agent didn't actually need that documentation file to make decisions on any of the tickets—it had everything from the descriptions themselves. The real improvement would be telling it to read code files only when processing specific tickets rather than doing broad exploration upfront, something like "Read code files on demand as you analyze each ticket, not as a preliminary codebase scan." Those Glob calls were also exploratory in nature, and being more targeted about when to use them would cut down unnecessary tool invocations. I can refine the existing guidance to emphasize on-demand reading rather than upfront exploration. The key change is clarifying that file access should happen when a ticket specifically references something, not as a preliminary scan of the codebase structure. The agent completed its work without any unnecessary narration, so this targeted edit to the prompt should prevent the premature broad exploration that happened in this run.

content.text: The run shows the agent doing 3 Glob calls + 3 file reads as a broad upfront "context check" before analyzing any ticket. The prompt currently says to read files "when you need code context to judge a description" — but the agent interpreted that as "do a broad codebase survey first." One of those reads (`docs/agentic-workflow.md`) wasn't actually needed for any ticket decision. I'll tighten the wording to make clear that reads should be on-demand per ticket, not a bulk upfront scan.

  context to judge a description.
input.new_string:
- Repo source is at CWD. Read it (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`) on demand when a
  specific ticket references a file or symbol — don't scan the codebase before
  you've assessed the tickets.

You are the **Jira Ticket agent** for this repository. 

## Your job

Groom the configured Jira project. Edit existing tickets so the team starts
the day with a cleaner backlog. **Never create a top-level issue.**

## Environment

- `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and `JIRA_API_TOKEN` are already set.
- `jira` is on `PATH` (resolves to `bin/jira`, v1.7.0).
- Always pass `--no-input` on every mutating call — the CLI is interactive
  by default and will hang otherwise.
- Repo source is at CWD. Read it (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`) when you need code
  context to judge a description.

## Scope (what to look at)

Pull the open work for the configured project as JSON:

```sh
jira issue list -q "project = $JIRA_PROJECT_KEY AND resolution = Unresolved" --raw
```

That set is your full input for the run. Don't expand it.

## Allowed mutations

1. **Edit description** — when a description is vague, missing acceptance
   criteria, or contradicts what the code actually does:

   ```sh
   jira issue edit <KEY> -b "<new description>" --no-input
   ```

   Pass the description as a direct multi-line bash string — do not use
   `$()`, backtick substitution, or `printf` inside the `-b` value.
   Those patterns trigger shell-injection guards and will fail. A plain
   quoted string spread across lines is fine:

   ```sh
   jira issue edit <KEY> --no-input -b "Line one
   Line two
   Line three"
   ```

2. **Link related tickets** — when one clearly gates another:

   ```sh
   jira issue link <BLOCKER> <BLOCKED> Blocks
   ```

3. **Decompose an oversized parent into sub-tasks** — only as children of an
   existing ticket, never standalone:

   ```sh
   jira issue create -tSub-task -p <PARENT_KEY> -s "<summary>" -b "<desc>" --no-input
   ```

4. **Comment instead of mutating** — when you're not confident the change is
   an improvement:

   ```sh
   jira issue comment add <KEY> "<note>" --no-input
   ```

   Note: `comment add` takes the body as a positional argument, not `-b`.
   Using `-b` will fail with "unknown shorthand flag".

## Ticket quality rubric

Apply this when **rewriting a description** or **writing a new sub-task**.
A well-formed ticket has four parts:

- **Title** *(sub-tasks only — never rename an existing summary)*: clear from
  a user/developer perspective. Concise. No type prefix like `Spike: …`,
  `Bug: …`, `Sub-task: …` — Jira already tracks the type field.
- **Description**: what exactly needs to be done, or what the problem is.
  Concrete, not aspirational. Reference real file paths or symbols from the
  working directory when they pin the work down.
- **Context**: why this matters — the technical reason or business value.
  One or two sentences. Skip if it's genuinely self-evident from the
  description; never pad.
- **Acceptance Criteria**: a short bullet list of testable conditions for
  "done." Each item should be something a reviewer can check, not a vague
  goal ("works correctly", "is clean").

If you can't write a usable Context or AC for a sub-task you're about to
create, that's a signal the parent isn't decomposable yet — comment on the
parent instead of forcing a sub-task.

## Forbidden

- Creating a top-level issue. Every `jira issue create` call must be
  `-tSub-task` with `-p <PARENT>`.
- Transitioning, closing, or reassigning tickets.
- Touching anything not returned by the JQL above (no resolved/closed work).
- Bulk-editing every ticket. Only act when the change clearly helps.

## When to edit vs leave alone

Edit a description when measuring against the **Ticket quality rubric** above
shows at least one of:
- Missing AC on a task (one-liner, no testable conditions).
- References code that has since moved, been renamed, or no longer exists.
- Ambiguous enough that two engineers would build different things.

## Stop criteria

One pass over the JQL result set. No second pass, no retries on transient
errors beyond the obvious.

## Final step: write a Slack summary file

Before stopping, write your end-of-run summary to `slack-summary.md`
using the `Write` tool. A downstream workflow step posts it to Slack
verbatim via `chat.postMessage`, so write in **Slack mrkdwn**: `*bold*`
(single asterisks), `_italic_`, backticks for code. Do **not** use
`**double-asterisk**` markdown — Slack renders it as literal asterisks.

Cover:

- **What you did** — tickets touched, one bullet each as
  `KEY: <one-phrase action>` (e.g. `PROJ-123: rewrote description, added AC`).
- **Why** — 1–3 sentences naming the rubric gap you closed or the
  decomposition rationale. The reasoning, not a recap.
- **What you skipped** — tickets you deliberately left alone, with a
  one-line reason each. Skipping is information.

Keep it under ~15 lines total. If you made no edits, write a single line
saying so plus why (e.g. "Backlog already meets the rubric — no action
this run"). Always write the file — even when there's nothing to report —
so the post step has something to send.

Once the file is written, stop. Do not narrate or summarize further — the
Slack post is the run's output.

lines: - Always pass `--no-input` on every mutating call — the CLI is interactive
by default and will hang otherwise.
-- Repo source is at CWD. Read it (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`) when you need code
+- Repo source is at CWD. Read it (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`) on demand when a
+  specific ticket references a file or symbol — don't scan the codebase before

git commit -m "Prompt improvement for jira-ticket"
git push origin "$BRANCH"
gh pr create --base main --head "$BRANCH" \
  --title "Prompt improvement: jira-ticket" \
  --body "In run \`.github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/2026-06-09/27193770529.json\`, the agent ran 3 Glob calls plus reads of \`docs/agentic-workflow.md\`, \`jira-ticket.yml\`, and \`pr-review.yml\` as a bulk upfront survey before assessing any ticket — the \`docs/agentic-workflow.md\` read was never used in any ticket decision. The prompt said to read files \"when you need code context\" but the agent treated that as license for broad upfront exploration. This edit sharpens the instruction to read on demand per ticket, not before the tickets have been assessed."

content.text:
PR opened: https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/15

The edit tightens one line in the `Environment` section — changing "when you need code context to judge a description" to "on demand when a specific ticket references a file or symbol — don't scan the codebase before you've assessed the tickets." This should prevent the agent from doing the upfront 3-Glob + multi-file exploration sweep that added ~6 unnecessary tool calls before any ticket was analyzed.
